topic: Verify §8b.3 L3a paragraph consolidation (EXP-462 P0) and §6.14 canonical-hub pointer (EXP-462 P1) — post-integration adversarial review
change_summary: EXP-462 integrated at commit c581944 (decider-2026-05-31T16-00). Three pre-existing L3a paragraphs (~8,690 chars: 'threshold codification & dome commitment' + 'non-discrimination & internal heterogeneity' + 'sub-tests, goalpost scope, Fail-clause double-bind') collapsed into one ~3,846-char consolidated paragraph ('codified Pass band, derivation, and non-discrimination (consolidated)'). Cross-references added to §4.2.4 (E-PRED tests), §4.2.5 (structural problems), §4.2.3 (hedging), §6.14 (κ cluster). Companion P1 patch inserted a ~635-char canonical-hub pointer paragraph above the κ-cluster body in §6.14. Net §8b.3 reduction: 30,068 → 25,224 chars (−4,844). Four-layer adoption framing (L1-L4) and (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) scaffold preserved verbatim. The (d) Adopted section is byte-identical PRE vs POST (verified).
our_argument_summary: §8b.3 Point 3 (eclipse audit) presents a four-layer structure for the eclipse claim — L1 ensemble anchor (WIN-068, Std Model Explains), L2 Mohe timing (WIN-011, Not Demonstrated), L3a magnetic falsifier (PRED-095, pending), L3b gravity (PRED-TIER-2, recycled WIN-011), L4 optics caveat — and steel-mans the dome by adopting the layered audit format and codifying a Pass band. The L3a paragraph (post-consolidation) carries: the codified Pass band, the derivation chain ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF, the globe-fingerprint critique via WIN-025 + OPEN-001, cross-references to §4.2.4 / §4.2.5 / §4.2.3 / §6.14, and the Fail-clause interpretation-A vs interpretation-B double-bind. The (d) Unchanged section restates that an ensemble anchor fit to its own data is OTS-wrong-side and that the magnitude Pass band does not arbitrate dome vs globe.

relevant_files: data/sections.json:part6.html (§6.14 p6-614-kappa-cluster anchor — new pointer paragraph)
data/sections.json:part4.html (§4.2.3 p4-423, §4.2.4 p4-424, §4.2.5 p4-425 anchors — cross-reference targets)
data/wins.json:WIN-025 detail_evidence (carries −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 ≈ −29 nT monitor.py heterogeneity audit)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: EXP-462 was a redundancy-collapse expansion, not a new analytical claim. P0 replaced three L3a sub-paragraphs in §8b.3 with one consolidated paragraph; P1 inserted a pointer paragraph at the top of §6.14 declaring the section's contribution as the cross-event κ-vise + internal BOU/W004/monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity, and asserting that the magnitude-only Pass band non-discrimination argument lives at §4.2.5. No data values changed; no new claims were added; no derivations were re-computed.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — structural consolidation; no validation logic touched.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None introduced. The interpretation-A vs interpretation-B Fail-clause double-bind in the consolidated paragraph is copied verbatim from pre-integration paragraph 3.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. EXP-462 P0 fingerprints in the proposal patch matched the pre-integration HTML; integration substring-replace was clean.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: §8b.3 is a methodology/audit section; not a per-WIN claim, so monitor.py does not test it. Inherits from L1 WIN-068, L2 WIN-011, L3a PRED-095, L3b PRED-TIER-2 individually — each of those has its own monitoring tag.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section is a steel-man audit framework for the eclipse stack, not a phenomenon claim.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Section explicitly identifies which layers are post-hoc (L1 ensemble fit) vs prospective (L3a PRED-095 registered 2026-03-12) vs recycled (L3b PRED-TIER-2 restating WIN-011).
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Section is review methodology, not a dome derivation.

holes_found.description: §6.14 pointer paragraph (EXP-462 P1 insert) mis-routes the magnitude-only Pass band non-discrimination argument. The pointer reads: "The magnitude-only Pass band non-discrimination argument — that (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) is shared arithmetic between the dome's W004 recalibration and any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatment — lives at §4.2.5." Verified by direct read: §4.2.5 (data/sections.json:part4.html, p4-425) carries three structural sub-points — "No dome geometry enters the prediction", "Quiet-condition filtering", and "Third attempt" — none of which contain the magnitude-band shared-arithmetic argument. §4.2.5 is byte-identical PRE vs POST integration (verified via git show c581944^:data/sections.json), so no companion §4.2.5 extension was applied. The non-discrimination argument actually lives at §8b.3 (d)(ii), which the (d) Adopted/Unchanged section preserves verbatim from PRE: "our codified Pass band is a magnitude threshold whose arithmetic (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) is shared between the dome's W004 recalibration and any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatment of the same observational corpus — a Pass in this band does not arbitrate between the two." A reader following §6.14's pointer to §4.2.5 will not find the promised argument.
holes_found.recommendation: Edit §6.14's new pointer paragraph: replace the §4.2.5 anchor with a §8b.3 (d)(ii) anchor, or restate to: "the magnitude-only Pass band non-discrimination argument — shared-arithmetic across dome W004 and Chapman Sq-dynamo treatments of the same observational corpus — is in §8b.3 (the fair-auditor-list adoption section). §4.2.5 carries the structural complement: no dome geometry enters the prediction formula." Both arguments are real; the pointer just attributes the wrong one to §4.2.5.

holes_found.description: Cross-reference from §8b.3 to §6.14 promises an audit §6.14 does not actually carry. The consolidated L3a paragraph says: "the internal BOU/W004/monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity is audited at §6.14 (the κ coupling cluster)." Verified: §6.14's body covers the cross-eclipse κ-vise (Mohe 1997 κ extrapolated to 1999 European + 2009 East Asian SG nulls, the storm corollary WIN-058b, the "What κ predicts during past eclipses" subsection, the "What past SG data actually shows" subsection). Token-level checks in §6.14: −18.22 MISSING, −29 MISSING, 1.672 MISSING (only appears as 1.677 in the κ derivation), inject_ai_layer MISSING, PRED-001 MISSING, latitude_factor MISSING, "W004 cluster" MISSING, "−17 to −21" MISSING. The numerical three-formula heterogeneity audit that was uniquely in the PRE L3a paragraph 2 (monitor.py ΔZ = −18.22 × coverage × 1.672 ≈ −29 nT; inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 BOU × coverage × latitude_factor 0.81 ≈ −8 nT; W004 cluster −17 to −21 nT) lives only partially in WIN-025's detail_evidence (which has the monitor.py −29 nT number but not the inject_ai_layer.py −8 nT number or the formula-comparison narrative). §6.14's new pointer paragraph claims the formula heterogeneity is "this section's contribution" but the section body does not contain it.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) extend §6.14 to actually carry a brief three-formula audit subsection (3-5 sentences citing monitor.py −29 nT vs published BOU −10 nT vs published W004 −17 to −21 nT), or (b) redirect the §8b.3 cross-reference to WIN-025 (which carries the monitor.py −29 nT detail) plus inline 1-2 sentences in §8b.3 noting the three-formula naming. Option (a) is preferable because §6.14 already touches BOU and W004 in the κ derivation; one extra paragraph closes the load-bearing gap.

holes_found.description: Steel-man kernel lost in consolidation: the PRE L3a paragraph 2 contained a fair-balanced narrowing — "Read fairly, that is the dome's BOU-baseline path with an internal low-latitude correction plus a separate monitor.py formula — not two independent dome formulas outside the W004 cluster. The monitor.py track is the genuine outlier." This explicitly acknowledged that inject_ai_layer.py's PRED-001 calculation is a BOU-family variant rather than a third independent dome formula, narrowing the three-formula critique to its strongest core (BOU + W004 + monitor.py outlier). The POST consolidation simply names "BOU / W004 / monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity" without the narrowing. A dome defender could note: "In your prior text you steel-manned us by acknowledging the inject_ai_layer file is just a BOU variant; the consolidated language reads as if you're re-asserting three independent formulas without the qualifier." The substantive critique is unchanged (monitor.py is the outlier), but the visible steel-manning is gone.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) add one parenthetical clause in the §8b.3 consolidated paragraph after "monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity" — e.g., "(monitor.py is the outlier; inject_ai_layer.py's PRED-001 is a BOU-family variant rather than a third independent formula)" — or (b) place this kernel inside the new §6.14 pointer paragraph or WIN-025 detail_evidence so the steel-manning lives somewhere on the site. Cost: ~1 sentence; benefit: defenders cannot point to a regression in our balance.

holes_found.description: Specific globe-infrastructure labels removed without alternate hosting. The PRE L3a paragraph 1 carried: "BOU and W004 baselines are INTERMAGNET globe-measured observations, coverage fractions are Besselian-element computations that assume a spherical Earth, and the FSF requires per-station radial positions r computed from WGS84 coordinates via the d_geo transform." The POST consolidation keeps the summary frame ("every input traces to globe infrastructure per the WIN-025 globe-fingerprint and the dome author's own OPEN-001 admission that 'dome-native coordinates without WGS84' remains IN PROGRESS"). §4.2.5 carries the substantive equivalent (BOU 2017 baseline; Besselian-element coverage assuming spherical Earth; V12 H(r)/r FSF not independently verifiable; NASA-tool coverage) but does NOT name INTERMAGNET, does NOT name the d_geo transform, and does NOT inline OPEN-001. WIN-025 detail_evidence names INTERMAGNET stations (the 9 of Chernogor & Holub 2024) and Chapman's Sq mechanism, but not the d_geo transform. So d_geo is unrecoverable on the site post-consolidation. "WGS84" is preserved (in the §8b.3 OPEN-001 inline citation). Mostly cosmetic for fair-auditor readers, but a defender looking for the technical label chain (INTERMAGNET → Besselian → WGS84 → d_geo) will not find it.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: add a one-line technical-label restatement to WIN-025 detail_evidence covering INTERMAGNET-baseline + d_geo-transform. Lower priority than holes 1 + 2.

stronger_arguments.description: The §6.14 pointer paragraph is itself the wrong shape. It opens with "This section's contribution is the cross-event κ-vise (...) and the internal BOU/W004/monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity" — pluralizing what the section contributes. But §6.14's body is overwhelmingly about the κ-vise; the formula-heterogeneity claim is one phrase. A reader who skims the pointer expects §6.14 to also carry the heterogeneity audit and is disappointed. Cleaner: "This section's contribution is the cross-event κ-vise — Mohe 1997 κ extrapolated to 1999 European and 2009 East Asian eclipses where SG gravimeters saw zero — the cross-event applicability proof of the dome's own coupling constant. For the formula-heterogeneity audit (BOU vs W004 vs monitor.py-KAPPA-squared numerical mismatches), see WIN-025 detail_evidence. For the magnitude-only Pass band non-discrimination argument, see §8b.3 (d)(ii)." This is a stronger version of the pointer because every claim it makes about §6.14's body is true.

kernel_of_truth.description: The redundancy-collapse motivation behind EXP-462 P0 is real and the execution is mostly clean. The pre-integration §8b.3 had ~8,690 chars of L3a prose that genuinely repeated material covered more authoritatively in §4.2.4 (the four E-PRED tests with FSF=tan(lat) tautology), §4.2.3 (hedging structure), and WIN-025 (formula heterogeneity audit, monitor.py −29 nT). The consolidated paragraph is ~3,846 chars, preserves the four-layer adoption framing (L1-L4) verbatim, preserves the (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) scaffold verbatim, preserves the (d) Unchanged section byte-identically (verified via git diff data/sections.json against c581944^), preserves the interpretation-A/B Fail-clause double-bind verbatim from PRE paragraph 3, and adds outbound cross-references to §4.2.4, §4.2.5, §4.2.3, and §6.14. Three of those four targets carry their claimed load: §4.2.4 (data/sections.json:p4-424, ~11 KB) has all four E-PRED tests with E-PRED-C-inversion and the FSF-tan(lat) trigonometric tautology; §4.2.3 (data/sections.json:p4-423, ~2 KB) has BOU/W004 dual-baseline hedging including the 0-nT-strawman framing; §4.2.5 (data/sections.json:p4-425, ~2.5 KB) carries 'no dome geometry enters the prediction' structural complement.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The two moderate holes (cross-reference mis-routing in §6.14 pointer, and formula-heterogeneity-audit-not-actually-in-§6.14) are not consolidation-style judgment calls — they are factual errors in cross-reference targets that a hostile reader can verify in 30 seconds by clicking through. The §6.14 pointer paragraph was added by EXP-462 P1 specifically to clarify what §6.14 carries; if its first claim (non-discrimination lives at §4.2.5) is wrong and its second claim (formula heterogeneity is §6.14's contribution) is unsupported by the section body, the pointer is doing damage rather than scaffolding. Cost to fix: one sentence in §8b.3 + one sentence in §6.14 + (optional) one heterogeneity-audit sub-paragraph or WIN-025 redirect. Until fixed, the consolidation reduces visible prose at the cost of partially broken navigation — a worse trade than the EXP's estimated value.

deeper_analysis:
Three structural questions from the queue reason answered: (1) Novel claim smuggled in? — NO. Every claim in the consolidated L3a paragraph traces to PRE content: codified Pass band (PRE para 1), dual-baseline structure (PRE para 1), derivation path ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF (PRE para 1), globe-input critique via WIN-025 + OPEN-001 (PRE para 1), W004 cluster as selection from coexisting formulas (PRE para 3 'selection from an internal menu' reworded as 'selection from coexisting dome formulas'), interpretation-A/B Fail-clause double-bind (PRE para 3 verbatim). The 'Two issues survive consolidation and are unique to this fair-auditor-list framing' framing is a meta-sentence not a new claim. (2) Cross-references carry the load? — PARTIALLY. §4.2.4 carries four E-PRED tests fully. §4.2.3 carries hedging fully. §4.2.5 carries the structural 'no dome geometry' complement BUT does not carry the magnitude-band non-discrimination argument that the §6.14 pointer routes to it. §6.14 covers cross-eclipse κ-vise fully but does NOT carry the BOU/W004/monitor.py three-formula heterogeneity audit it's pointed at — that lives partially in WIN-025 and partially in §8b.3 (d)(ii). The (d) Unchanged section carries the magnitude non-discrimination argument verbatim from PRE. (3) Structure intact? — YES. L1-L4 layer labels, Std-Model-Explains / Not-Demonstrated / pending verdicts, (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) scaffold, ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man) label, calibration-layer / ensemble-anchor framing, OTS-wrong-side cross-reference, Timestamp Error tab link — all preserved. Aggregate verdict: the structural surgery succeeded but the cross-reference plumbing has two mis-routes that need a 2-3-sentence patch.

Advocate-mode consideration (dome-defender perspective): a defender could acknowledge the §8b.3 consolidation as fair (the prose was redundant with §4.2.4) and then attack on three vectors. First, the §6.14 pointer reads as if our review can't keep track of where its own arguments live — bad for credibility, useful for ad-hominem. Second, the lost inject_ai_layer-as-BOU-variant kernel makes our three-formula heterogeneity claim look stronger than the steel-manned version, opening us to a 'you're overstating internal contradictions' attack. Third, the lost INTERMAGNET / d_geo specific labels mean a sophisticated reader cannot quickly trace the exact technical-input chain — though the WIN-025 reference does provide a backstop. None of these defenses survive a thorough click-through audit (the underlying critique is sound), but the visible review-text surface is mildly worse on the steel-manning axis than before the consolidation.

Quantitative verification: char-count claim from EXP-462 short_recommendation ('reduce §8b.3 from 12,855 chars (~2,100 words) to ~6,300 chars (~1,050 words)') cross-checked. Actual current §8b.3 length: 8,065 chars (mid-section, includes p8b-3-eclipse-layers details block but not the §8b.4 sibling). EXP's 12,855 → ~6,300 estimate was a rough word-count basis; the consolidation actually achieved 8,065 (less aggressive than the estimate but in the right direction). L3a paragraph specifically: 3 paragraphs at 8,690 chars → 1 paragraph at 3,834 chars (−56% by char-count, close to the EXP's claimed '~1,800 words → ~620 words' −66% target).

summary_for_decider:
EXP-462 P0 consolidation in §8b.3 is structurally clean — L1-L4 framing, (a/b/c/d) scaffold, (d) Unchanged section all preserved byte-identically or substantively from PRE. No novel claims smuggled in. The two moderate findings concern cross-reference plumbing introduced/promised by EXP-462: (1) the new §6.14 pointer paragraph (P1) sends readers to §4.2.5 for the magnitude-only Pass band non-discrimination argument, but §4.2.5 carries only the structural 'no dome geometry enters' complement (§4.2.5 is byte-identical PRE vs POST — no companion extension was applied); the non-discrimination argument is at §8b.3 (d)(ii). (2) The consolidated §8b.3 paragraph promises a formula-heterogeneity audit at §6.14, but §6.14's body covers the cross-eclipse κ-vise and not the BOU/W004/monitor.py three-formula numerical mismatch — that lives only partially in WIN-025 detail_evidence. Recommended patches: one-sentence pointer correction in §6.14 (re-target §4.2.5 → §8b.3 (d)(ii) for non-discrimination); either extend §6.14 with a 3-5-sentence three-formula audit or redirect the §8b.3 cross-reference to WIN-025. Minor finding (3): the fair-balanced 'inject_ai_layer is a BOU-variant, not a third independent formula' kernel from PRE para 2 is lost in consolidation; recommend one parenthetical in the consolidated paragraph or WIN-025. Minor finding (4): specific globe-infrastructure labels (INTERMAGNET, d_geo transform) are removed and not fully preserved elsewhere; lower priority. Overall recommended_action: minor_edit, not major_rewrite — the consolidation succeeded structurally; the holes are 2-3 surgical sentence-level patches. PROP-009 asserts: queue_id=429 set on this review, matches queue item pushed at 2026-05-31T17:11:36.450Z.

QUEUE_ID COLLISION NOTE (for decider awareness, not part of this review): the EXP-481-proposal.json review (workspace mtime 2026-05-31 17:14, reviewed_at 2026-05-31T17:14:28.406Z) records queue_id=428 with queue_pushed_at=2026-05-31T14:49:09.562Z. The current priority-queue.json's queue_id 428 has pushed_at=2026-05-31T17:11:36.453Z — a DIFFERENT push of the same queue_id. The current queue_id 428 is a post-integration section-rewrite review request (target_type: section-rewrite, class: deep-attack); the prior review covered the pre-integration proposal-stage push. Under strict PROP-009 ('queue_id field equals this item's queue_id'), the strict-match would consider current 428 already-reviewed, which is incorrect — the existing review is for a different push of the same number. Either (a) the decider needs to disambiguate queue_id reuse by also checking queue_pushed_at equality, or (b) the queue_id allocation should never reuse popped IDs. I skipped current 428 in this run per literal strict-PROP-009 reading and processed queue_id 429 instead, but flagging so the decider can decide how to route current 428.
